form_name: Disclosure Regarding Real Estate Agency Relationship (Buyer Side)
notes: Buyer-side schema. Seller-side AD is handled by a separate schema (AD-SELLER-1224 — not yet built). All signatures are on the first page; page 2 contains only Civil Code text and the form has no entity-related fields.

audit_conventions.page_resolution: Each signature_location specifies a page_anchor — text patterns that identify the target page(s). The runtime extracts text from each PDF page and matches anchors against extracted text.
audit_conventions.filter_by_present_parties: Runtime only checks signatures for parties whose names exist in the extracted offer data.
audit_conventions.graceful_missing_line: If the runtime cannot find the expected line/field on a page, it returns 'not_applicable' rather than 'missing'.
audit_conventions.phase_filtering: Not state-dependent on this form. Buyer and agent both sign at the same moment when the agent presents the form to the buyer, so all locations use phase=buyer_offer.
audit_conventions.scenario_filtering: The AD form has no entity-specific fields, so no scenario filtering applies.

signature_locations.description: Buyer signature block on the acknowledgment page, in the section with the 'Buyer/Tenant' checkbox

any_of: I/WE ACKNOWLEDGE RECEIPT OF A COPY OF THIS DISCLOSURE

signature_locations.description: Date field next to each buyer signature on the acknowledgment page

any_of: I/WE ACKNOWLEDGE RECEIPT OF A COPY OF THIS DISCLOSURE

signature_locations.description: Agent signature on the acknowledgment page, above the line labeled 'Salesperson or Broker-Associate, if any'
signature_locations.notes: Watch for typed name vs actual signature glyph — a printed name in the line area without a signature mark should be flagged for review, not auto-cleared.

signature_locations.description: Date field next to the buyer agent signature on the acknowledgment page
